2606.08210.title: Paediatric-HGNN: A Hybrid Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network for Detecting Disfluency in Children's Speech via Multiscale Acoustic Fusion
2606.08210.paper_summary: Automated stuttering detection (ASD) systems struggle with paediatric speech due to high acoustic variability in developing voices and the subtle distinction between pathological stuttering and typical developmental disfluencies. We introduce Paediatric-HGNN, a framework using a Context-aware Part-whole Interaction Network (CaPIN) tailored for paediatric data. Instead of conventional 1D signal modelling, our approach builds a heterogeneous graph capturing hierarchical relationships between lexical units (word nodes) and fine-grained acoustic segments (frame nodes). Trained on curated paediatric corpora (UCLASS and FluencyBank), Paediatric-HGNN achieves 82.4% weighted accuracy and a Typical Disfluency F1-score of 0.386. Modelling hierarchical lexical-acoustic interactions captures developmental "searching" behaviour, offering a more robust and interpretable tool for early clinical intervention.
2606.08210.paper_summary_zh: 自動口吃檢測（ASD）系統在兒童語音中面臨挑戰，因為發展中聲音的聲學變異性高，以及病理性口吃與典型發展性流暢性障礙之間的微妙區別。我們介紹了兒童-HGNN，這是一個使用上下文感知的部分整體互動網絡（CaPIN）為兒童數據量身定制的框架。與傳統的1D信號建模不同，我們的方法構建了一個異質圖，捕捉詞彙單位（詞節點）與細粒度聲學片段（幀節點）之間的層次關係。在經過精心挑選的兒童語料庫（UCLASS和FluencyBank）上訓練後，兒童-HGNN達到了82.4%的加權準確率和0.386的典型流暢性F1分數。建模層次詞彙-聲學互動捕捉了發展中的“搜尋”行為，為早期臨床干預提供了一個更穩健且可解釋的工具。
2606.08210.author: Rashini Liyanarachchi et.al.
2606.08210.authors: Rashini Liyanarachchi, Rachael Mackay, Alison Short, Aditya Joshi, Erik Meijering
